## Transforming Text into a Graph

## Transformation - Standardization

```
[**GREGORY**]{#speech24} > [I will frown as I pass by, and let them take it as]{#1.1.36}\ > [they list.]{#1.1.37}\ [**SAMPSON**]{#speech25} > [Nay, as they dare. I will bite my thumb at them;]{#1.1.38}\ > [which is a disgrace to them, if they bear it.]{#1.1.39}\ > *Enter ABRAHAM and BALTHASAR* [**ABRAHAM**]{#speech26}
```

- &gt; [Do you bite your thumb at us, sir?]{#1.1.40}\

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

24, "I will frown as I pass by, and let them take it as they list.", "GREGORY", "GREGORY, SAMPSON"

25, "Nay, as they dare. I will bite my thumb at them which is a disgrace to them, if they bear it", "SAMPSON", , "GREGORY, SAMPSON"

26, Do you bite your thumb at us, sir?", "ABRAHAM", , "GREGORY, SAMPSON, ABRAHAM, BALTHASAR"

## Transformation - Data Augmentation

Who is participating in the conversation

- [**SAMPSON**]{#speech25} &gt; [Nay, as they dare. I will bite my thumb at them;]{#1.1.38}\ &gt; [which is a disgrace to them, if they bear
- it.]{#1.1.39}\

## &gt; *Enter ABRAHAM and BALTHASAR*

```
[**ABRAHAM**]{#speech26}
```

- &gt; [Do you bite your thumb at us, sir?]{#1.1.40}\

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

New column/feature in the dataset

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

SAMPSON

SAMPSON, ABRAHAM and BALTHASAR

n - Filter

## Feature Selection - Filter

Filtering Emotions

L

Output feeling

The output feeling for the text is "Feeling disrespected".

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

Input text

The input text for the

image is "Do you bite

your thumb at us,

sir?".
